news.title: Meet the referees for the France-Spain 2026 World Cup Semifinal at Dallas Stadium
news.lede: Soccer Football – FIFA World Cup 2026 – Round of 16 – Switzerland v Colombia – BC Place, Vancouver, Canada – July 7, 2026 Referee Ivan Barton blows his whistle REUTERS/Lee Smith. The 2026 FIFA World Cup wraps up at Dallas Stadium on Tuesday at 2 p.m. when France plays Spain in the first Semifinal. Refereeing has made headlines this tournament, including the quarterfinals. Switzerland was outraged after referee João Pinheiro reversed a yellow card decision in the second half against Argentina that ended up sending a Swiss player off instead - it's a new rule FIFA calls "mistaken identity."

news.title: Dutch referee dies weeks after being dropped from World Cup
news.lede: Rob Dieperink began refereeing in the Netherlands' top flight in 2017. Dutch football referee Rob Dieperink has died weeks after he was dropped from officiating at the World Cup following a police investigation in the UK. Dieperink had been selected to be a video assistant referee (VAR) official at this summer's tournament, but was removed from Fifa's list of World Cup officials in May. The 38-year-old was arrested by the Metropolitan Police in April following a report of a sexual assault against a teenage boy, but the case was dropped because of a lack of evidence.

news.title: Spain, France, Argentina and England beware: Demons haunt the World Cup semi-finals
news.lede: The World Cup spotlight causes single games to live longer in the memory than any other. The remaining teams at this tournament face defining moments. World Cup games mean more. England have only ever played 79 games in the finals, which is to say, not much more than two Premier League seasons in the 76 years since they first entered.

news.title: World Cup semifinals. Who made final 4 teams, schedule, odds
news.lede: For the first time in history, the top four teams in the FIFA world rankings are all in the World Cup semifinals, as France, Spain, Argentina and England avoided the upsets that defined the early part of the tournament. Spain got off to a slow start in the World Cup, England struggled in the middle of the group stage, Argentina needed a miraculous escape in the round of 16, while France has rolled for the most part, and now all are two wins away from hoisting the trophy. More: What a World Cup sounds like in Texas. Sights from England-Croatia.

news.title: World Cup 2026 Predictions for Semifinals Bracket Based on Latest Odds
news.lede: The largest World Cup in history has reached its penultimate stage. What began as a 48-nation field is down to its final four. This week, the final match will be set.

news.title: 2026 World Cup: All the European sides' group stage and knockout stage fixtures and results
news.lede: Keep track of all the European sides' fixtures and results at the World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States. The 2026 FIFA World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States kicked off on 11 June and concludes with the final in East Rutherford on 19 July. Check out all the details of the European teams' matches. Wednesday 17 June: W 4-2 vs Croatia (Dallas) Tuesday 23 June: D 0-0 vs Ghana (Boston) Saturday 27 June: W 2-0 vs Panama (New York) Wednesday 1 July (R32): W 2-1 vs DR Congo (Atlanta) Monday 6 July (R16): W 3-2 vs Mexico (Mexico City) Saturday 11 July (QF): W 2-1 aet vs Norway (Miami) Wednesday 15 July (SF): vs Argentina (Atlanta, 21:00).

news.title: Record prize money makes Fifa World Cup richest tournament in history
Slaven Bilic appointed Croatia coach for second spell after World Cup exit
news.lede: Bilic, a former central defender, spent six years as Croatia coach between 2006 and 2012, leading them in two European Championship finals.
